concerns.text: FR-003 requires creating 7 subsamples representing '[deferred], [deferred]...' of the full dataset. This contradicts the 'Methodology sketch' in the Idea Md and the 'Assumptions' section which explicitly define these levels as '5%, 10%, 20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, 100%'. The spec fails to resolve these specific values, leaving the requirement untestable and inconsistent with the project's defined scope.

User Story 2 Acceptance Scenario 1 lists 7 subsets as '[deferred], [deferred]...' without defining the percentages. This creates a contradiction with the 'Methodology sketch' (Idea Md) which specifies the exact levels (5%-100%). The spec must either adopt the specific values from the methodology or explicitly state that the levels are to be determined later, but currently it presents a placeholder where a defined scope is required for the '7 defined sparsity levels' mentioned in the story title.

The Assumptions section states: 'The Materials Project dataset contains sufficient formation energy entries (>150k) to support the [deferred] sparsity level.' The term '[deferred]' is used here as a placeholder for a specific percentage (likely the lowest, e.g., 5%), but the sentence structure implies a missing value. It should read 'support the lowest sparsity level (e.g., 5%)' or similar to maintain consistency with the defined levels in the methodology.

The Edge Case 'How does system handle out-of-memory errors during GPR training on [deferred] data?' uses '[deferred]' as a placeholder for a data size or sparsity level. This is inconsistent with the rest of the document where specific levels (5%-100%) are discussed in the methodology. It should specify 'on the largest sparsity level (100%)' or 'on the full dataset' to be actionable.
